utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items required in the format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All five appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with required details: each must include coverage type plus costs. 1) health_coverage: includes plan name and monthly_cost, but does not specify both physical and mental explicitly; however plan name references mental health and implies physical, and cost is provided—acceptable for costs. 2) property_coverage: includes plan name and monthly_cost; indicates home/contents insurance—cost provided. 3) travel_coverage: includes plan name and monthly_cost—cost provided. 4) additional_services: includes items with monthly_cost and features—costs provided. 5) total_budget: includes monthly_total and annual_total breakdown—provided. Therefore, all items are present with costs; despite some vagueness in feature detail, the required cost elements and category presence are satisfied.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan mentions specific plan names: LifeFirst Standard Plan (health), InsurePlus Mental Health Plan (interpreted as InsurePlus Standard Plan in mental health), HomeFirst Standard Coverage (property), TravelFirst Standard Plan (travel), Workplace Mental Health (as an additional service), and Specialist Access (additional service). I matched each to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings and extracted their exact ratings.
